title: Imposing Policy on Reluctant Actors: The Hospital Desegregation Campaign and Black Postneonatal Mortality in the Deep South

data.file: data/raw/262/RESTAT replication files/Data set for hospital desegregation analysis.dta

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. SA REMOVED (Pattern 49: cohorts extremely unbalanced — 1967=85%, 1970-73<1% each → IW variance explosion, CIs ±25). Bacon: 89% timing groups (11% TvU). CS-NT=0.98, CS-NYT=1.24 (SE 5x larger, NYT pool tiny for late cohorts). All estimators null. Anderson, Charles, Rees (2020) RESTAT. Stata: xtreg post_neonatal_rate medicare hs_deg_imp health_per_cap emp_to_pop i.year [pweight=births_ave] if race==3, fe i(state_county_fips) cluster(state_county_fips). Hospital desegregation via Medicare certification in the Deep South. Staggered treatment: counties certified 1967-1973 (7 cohorts). race==3 = Black. balanced==1 restricts to balanced panel. no_births==0 excludes zero-birth counties. Weight: average births (pweight). Event study: medi_lead/lag pre-computed in data. CS also estimated in paper using csdid with notyet, method(dripw). Controls are time-varying county covariates. | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor EXACT (0.08%). 12th Lesson 7 direct-level Spec A collapse (CS-NT+3 ctrls → 0/NA); CS-NYT+ctrls runs (2.72) but SE 5× inflated (underpowered — 1967 cohort 85% weight; late cohorts 1970-73 <1% each). TWFE robust OLS across specs (1.21 no ctrls vs 1.22 with ctrls). LOW rating driven by cohort imbalance + CS underpowered for late cohorts, not implementation.
